steps.description: Clarify what you want me to edit — I need the target change or which file(s) to modify. Possible meanings of 'try to edit again': retry a previously-failed edit_file operation, apply a new edit to some code, or re-run an automated agent that performs edits. Please tell me which file(s) and the intended change (or paste the original instruction you previously used).

Gather context needed to re-run or retry an edit. Read the Shipyard agent code, tools, hooks and related tests/docs so I understand how edits are performed and recorded (this is required before attempting another edit).

Locate previous edit attempts / failures so we can understand what to retry. Search the repository for references to recent agent edit activity or logs (fields like file_edits, edit_file usage, 'File edits:' in reporting). If you intended a retry of a specific earlier operation, point me to the output/error you observed.

After you clarify what edit to make (target files and exact changes), I'll create a concrete, deterministic edit plan: compute the exact anchors/old text to replace, the new content, and the fallbacks used by edit_file. I will list ALL files that will be modified and the exact changes to apply. (I will not modify anything until you confirm.)

If you confirm the edit and want me to proceed, I will: (a) run the surgical edit(s) using the edit_file tool; (b) update any related tests or docs if needed; (c) run the repository's relevant unit tests for Shipyard (locally) and surface failures for further fixes. I will show a summary of file edits and verification output. (Tell me to proceed or give the exact edit now.)
